example.gloss: What is your profession?
My sister is a doctor.
The nurse works in a hospital.

words.note: feminine: učiteljica; secondary school and university: profesor

example.gloss: Our teacher is very kind.
The teacher explains the new lesson.
The professor teaches mathematics.
My brother is an engineer.
The waiter is bringing two coffees.
The waitress works in a restaurant.
The cook is making a tasty soup.
The driver drives the bus around town.
The police officer is standing in the street.
The firefighter is putting out the fire.
I need a good lawyer.
The salesperson works in a shop.
The hairdresser is cutting my hair.
The baker bakes fresh bread.
The butcher sells fresh meat.
Tomorrow I'm going to the dentist.
The journalist writes for a newspaper.
The actor works in the theatre.
The singer sings a beautiful song.
The musician plays the guitar.
The athlete trains every day.
The farmer works in the countryside.
The fisherman catches fish at sea.
The secretary organizes the meetings.
The scientist works at the university.
The architect draws the house plan.

example.target: Programer cijeli dan radi na računalu.
example.gloss: The programmer works on the computer all day.
The entrepreneur has his own company.
The worker starts work at seven.

words.note: only for university; school pupil = učenik

example.gloss: The student is studying for an exam.
The student lives in Zagreb.
My grandfather is a pensioner.
The housewife cooks lunch for the family.
The soldier wears a uniform.
The priest works in our church.
The postman brings letters every day.
The taxi driver drives all over the city.

words.note: masculine: blagajnik; blagajna = cash register / till

example.gloss: The cashier is counting the money.
The director has a meeting at ten.

example.target: Brat mi je zaposlen u bolnici.
example.gloss: My brother is employed at the hospital.
He is unemployed at the moment.

words.note: followed by the nominative: radim kao konobar

example.gloss: I work as a waiter in a hotel.

words.note: grammatically feminine even when referring to a man

example.gloss: She is a very good person.
He is a smart boy.
That is a stupid idea.
The saleswoman is always kind.

words.note: Draga Ana = Dear Ana (letters); drag mi je = I like him

example.gloss: I like her a lot.
The film was very boring.
This book is very interesting.
The child is cheerful today.

words.note: ljut na + accusative; also means 'hot / spicy' (food)

example.gloss: Father is angry with me.
The mother is worried about her son.
I am proud of my children.
He is shy and quiet.
A good teacher is patient.
Children are impatient before a trip.
He is an honest man.
Be honest with me.
On weekends I am a bit lazy.
She is a hard-working student.
The director is a very serious man.
His story is very funny.
My friend is very witty.
Firefighters are brave people.

example.target: On je miran i tih čovjek.
example.gloss: He is a calm and quiet man.
Before an exam I am always nervous.
She is a quiet person.
The children are loud today.

example.target: Ona je otvorena i vesela osoba.
example.gloss: She is an open and cheerful person.
The new colleague is very likeable.
He is a handsome young man.
My brother is very tall.

example.target: Ona je niska, ali jako brza.
example.gloss: She is short but very fast.
That dog is very skinny.

example.target: Naš mačak je debeo i lijen.
example.gloss: Our tomcat is fat and lazy.

words.note: also of coffee/tea: jaka kava = strong coffee

example.gloss: A soldier must be strong.
Grandpa is old and weak.
I have a good feeling.

example.target: Ljubav je najvažnija stvar u životu.
example.gloss: Love is the most important thing in life.

words.note: strah od + genitive; Strah me je. = I'm scared.

example.gloss: I feel fear before the exam.
The children are jumping for joy.
Money cannot buy happiness.
His song is full of sadness.
I have a lot of worries at work.
Hope dies last. (proverb)

words.note: bojim se; takes the genitive: bojim se mraka

example.gloss: I am afraid of big dogs.
I am hoping for a good grade.

words.note: takes the dative: veselim se ljetu

example.gloss: I am looking forward to summer and the sea.
I get up every day at seven.
Yesterday I got up at six.
I wake up early in the morning.
I woke up at eight.
I take a shower every morning.
I brush my teeth every evening.
I get dressed quickly before work.

words.note: obučem se; imperative: obuci se; impf. partner: oblačiti se

example.gloss: Get dressed, we're going out!
For breakfast I have bread, cheese and an egg.

words.note: biaspectual, works as both impf. and pf.

example.gloss: On Sundays we have lunch at grandma's.

words.note: biaspectual, works as both impf. and pf.

example.gloss: We have dinner together at eight.

words.note: often reflexive: žuriti se; pf. partner: požuriti

example.gloss: I'm hurrying to work, see you!
The bus is ten minutes late again.

words.note: zakasniti na + accusative; impf. partner: kasniti

example.gloss: I was late for the meeting.
The train arrives at five o'clock.

words.note: stignem; past: stigao/stigla; impf. partner: stizati

example.gloss: We arrived home very late.
I come home around six.
I leave for work at eight.

words.note: odem; past: otišao/otišla; impf. partner: odlaziti

example.gloss: He left without saying goodbye.

example.target: Vraćam se s posla u pet.
example.gloss: I return from work at five.

example.target: Vratit ću se za pet minuta.
example.gloss: I'll be back in five minutes.
On weekends I rest at home.

words.note: družiti se s + instrumental; no pf. partner

example.gloss: I hang out with friends on Saturdays.

words.note: smijem se; pf. partner: nasmijati se

example.gloss: The children are laughing loudly.
The baby cries all night.

words.note: razgovarati s + instrumental; no pf. partner

example.gloss: I talk with my mum every day.

words.note: telefoniram; takes the dative; pf. 'to call': nazvati

example.gloss: I phone grandma every Sunday.
I'm sending you a message.
I sent the letter yesterday.
Every month I get a salary.
I got an excellent grade.
I keep losing my keys.
I lost my wallet on the tram.
I often forget people's names.
I forgot my homework at home.

words.note: takes the genitive; pf. partner: sjetiti se

example.gloss: I remember my first teacher.

words.note: takes the genitive; impf. partner: sjećati se

example.gloss: I remembered your name.
I try to study Croatian every day.
Try one more time!
I rarely manage to get up early.

words.note: uspijem; past: uspio/uspjela; impf. partner: uspijevati

example.gloss: I managed to pass the exam.
The film starts at eight.

example.target: Počeo sam učiti hrvatski prošle godine.
example.gloss: I started learning Croatian last year.
Work ends at five.
I finished my homework before dinner.

words.note: also the classroom group; prvi razred = first grade

example.target: Moj sin ide u prvi razred.
example.gloss: My son is in first grade.

words.note: the same word as sat 'hour/clock'; sat hrvatskog = Croatian class

example.gloss: The Croatian lesson starts at nine.
The pupil answers the question.
The pupil is writing her homework.
I am taking a Croatian language course.

words.note: distinctly Croatian (vs 'domaći zadatak'); also: domaća zadaća

example.gloss: I do my homework every day.
This task is very hard.

words.note: ispit iz + genitive: ispit iz matematike

example.gloss: Tomorrow I have a maths exam.

example.target: Dobio sam dobru ocjenu iz povijesti.
example.gloss: I got a good grade in history.
I write new words in my notebook.
I need a pencil and paper.
Do you have a pen?
The teacher writes on the board.
I look up a new word in the dictionary.
This translation is not correct.

example.target: Prevodim tekst s engleskog na hrvatski.
example.gloss: I am translating a text from English into Croatian.
What is the meaning of this word?

words.note: standard also: pogreška; genitive plural: grešaka

example.gloss: Everyone makes mistakes sometimes.
This exercise is not hard.
I practise Croatian every day.
Knowledge of languages opens many doors.
Learning a new language takes time.
The lecture lasts two hours.
My daughter studies at university.

example.target: Sveučilište u Zagrebu je jako staro.
example.gloss: The University of Zagreb is very old.

words.note: only for university; general studying = učiti; no pf. partner

example.gloss: I study medicine in Split.
My favourite subject is history.
Maths is hard for me.
History is an interesting subject.
Today we have geography and maths.
Croatian grammar has seven cases.
We are reading a short text in Croatian.
Open your books to page ten.
May I ask something?

words.note: odgovarati na + accusative; pf. partner: odgovoriti; also 'to suit'

example.gloss: The pupils answer the questions.
I answered all the questions.
We review the words before the exam.
Could you repeat that, please?
Can you explain this rule?
I borrow books at the library.
